segments.text: Tax collectors and other {
sinners
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: often used to come and listen to Jesus.
As a result the Pharisees and the religious teachers complained, {
This man welcomes sinners, and eats with them.
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: So Jesus told them this story as an illustration.
Imagine a man who had a hundred sheep lost one of them. Wouldn't he leave the ninety-nine in the open pasture, and search for the one that's lost until he finds it?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When he finds it, he joyfully puts it on his shoulders.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Once he gets home, he calls his friends and neighbors together, saying, ‘Come and celebrate with me! I've found my lost sheep!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you that there's more joy in heaven over a sinner that repents than over ninety-nine good people who don't need to repent.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Imagine a woman who has ten silver[^56] coins, and loses one of them. Wouldn't she light a lamp and sweep the house, carefully searching until she finds it?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When she finds it, she calls her friends and neighbors together, saying, ‘Come and celebrate with me! I've found the silver coin that I lost.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you there is joy in the presence of God's angels over one sinner that repents.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Once there was a man who had two sons,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus explained.
The younger one told his father, ‘Father, give me my inheritance now.’ So the man divided his property between them.
segments.speaker: Younger son

segments.text: A few days later the younger son packed up what he had and left for a distant country. Here he wasted all his money living a reckless life.
segments.speaker: Narrator

segments.text: After he'd spent everything, the country was hit by a severe famine and he was starving.
segments.speaker: Narrator

segments.text: So he went and took a job with one of the farmers there who sent him to his fields to feed pigs.
segments.speaker: Narrator

segments.text: He was so hungry that he would have eaten even the pig food, [^57] but no one gave him anything.
segments.speaker: Younger son

segments.text: When he came to his senses, he said to himself, ‘All of my father's workers have more than enough to eat—why am I dying from hunger here?
segments.speaker: Younger son

segments.text: I'm going home to my father! I'll tell him: Father, I've sinned against heaven and against you.
segments.speaker: Younger son

segments.text: I'm no longer worthy to be called your son. Please treat me as one of your hired workers.’
segments.speaker: Younger son

segments.text: So he left and went home to his father. Even though he was still far away in the distance, his father saw him coming, and his heart went out to his son. The father ran to his son, hugging and kissing him.
segments.speaker: Narrator

segments.text: The son said to him, ‘Father, I've sinned against heaven and against you. I'm no longer worthy to be called your son.’
segments.speaker: Younger son

segments.text: But the father told his servants, ‘Quick—bring the best robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet.
segments.speaker: The father

segments.text: Bring the calf we've been fattening and kill it. Let's have a feast to celebrate
segments.speaker: The father

segments.text: because this is my son who was dead, but who has returned alive; he was lost but now he's found.’ So they started celebrating.
segments.speaker: The father

segments.text: Now the older son was working out in the fields. As he walked towards the house, he heard music and dancing.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So he called one of the servants and asked what was going on.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: ‘Your brother is back,’ he replied, ‘and your father has killed the fattened calf, because he's come home safe and sound.’
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: The brother became angry. He refused to go in. So his father came out to plead with him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He told his father, ‘Look, all these years I've served you, and never disobeyed you, but you never once gave me even a young goat so I could have a party with my friends.
segments.speaker: The older son

segments.text: Now this son of yours comes back, having spent your money on prostitutes, and you kill the fattened calf for him!’
segments.speaker: The older son

segments.text: ‘Son,’ the father replied, ‘you are always here with me. Everything I have is yours.
segments.speaker: The father

segments.text: But we should be happy and celebrate! This is your brother who was dead, but who has returned alive; he was lost but now he's found!’
segments.speaker: The father
